sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Low back pain with right foot tingling and radicular symptoms.
History of Present Illness
The patient reports low back pain that began after raking leaves yesterday, accompanied by pins and needles in his right foot. He previously experienced similar pain three years ago, which resolved spontaneously. He has been largely sedentary and reports his husband does his yard work. He has not tried any treatments for the current pain. He describes his partner's pain tolerance as high and notes he would not be reporting the issue if it were truly severe. He suspects a long-term injury related to his husband's yard work.
Findings
Physical examination reveals normal dorsiflexion and equal pulses in all extremities. There is mild right foot tingling. Radiographs of the lumbar spine are unremarkable.
Assessment
Sprained low back.
Plan
Prescribe meloxicam, order physical therapy, and schedule a follow-up appointment in two weeks to reassess symptoms or consider an MRI if symptoms do not improve.
